changes: The scan recovered full GitHub metadata after yesterday's rate-limit fallback, so the latest snapshot now includes repo stats, release objects, and push timestamps across the tracked set.
OpenClaw published v2026.3.12 on 2026-03-13 and continues to ship on a rapid daily cadence.
Hermes Agent now exposes a `hermes claw migrate` command in its README and has a fresh v2026.3.12 release, making the OpenClaw-to-Hermes migration path more explicit.

SwarmClaw materially rewrote its README from an OpenClaw orchestration dashboard pitch toward a broader autonomous runtime / control-plane story with runtime skills, conversation-to-skill drafting, and crypto-wallet tooling.
Additional release activity is now visible across adjacent projects including ZeroClaw v0.1.9a, TinyClaw v0.0.12, AstrBot v4.20.0, and SwarmClaw v1.0.6.
whyItMatters: Yesterday's scan mostly confirmed structure; today's scan restores the market's actual movement layer.
Hermes is strengthening the competitive narrative that users can migrate from OpenClaw into a learning-loop agent rather than adopt a neighboring product cold.
SwarmClaw now looks less like a thin dashboard and more like an orchestration runtime ambition, which raises the strategic importance of the multi-agent control-plane lane.
The wider release burst reinforces that the category is still shipping fast across lightweight, orchestration, and adjacent platform players.
